title: Computer Systems Design Services

alternatives.name: Project-led computer systems integrator
inclusion_criteria: Employer firm is classified in NAICS 541512 and falls within the derived $1-10M EBITDA target band.

Project-based planning, design, configuration, implementation, or integration of computer hardware, packaged software, networks, security systems, and communication technologies is the firm's largest revenue category.
The firm may also provide support, managed services, consulting, or product resale, but none of those categories exceeds project-based systems-integration revenue.
exclusion_criteria: Recurring contracted management or technical support is the largest revenue category.
Stand-alone advisory, staff augmentation, or technical consulting is the largest revenue category.
Software publishing, intellectual-property licensing, hardware or software resale, hosting, or infrastructure provisioning is the largest revenue category.
No largest revenue category can be established, including an exact tie between categories.
alternatives.rationale: Base count is 44% of the normalized 3,524-firm band, rounded to the nearest firm. The 44% share is a dated, broader-channel survey estimate for firms naming IT solutions projects combining hardware, software, and services as their leading revenue source. Its ordering is corroborated, but not directly measured at the firm level, by the 2022 Economic Census: reported 541512 revenue in packaged-software customization/integration, network design/security, systems design/development, and systems integration totals about $153.9B, versus about $24.4B in infrastructure/network management, technical support, and hardware maintenance. Product lines can coexist within an establishment, and all-size revenue is not a target-band firm-count measure, so the supported count range is deliberately wide.

alternatives.name: Managed-services-led IT provider
inclusion_criteria: Employer firm is classified in NAICS 541512 and falls within the derived $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Recurring contracted IT infrastructure or network management, monitoring, maintenance, help-desk, or technical support is the firm's largest revenue category.
The firm may undertake systems-integration projects, consulting, or resale, but recurring managed and support services exceed each of those categories.
exclusion_criteria: Project-based systems design or integration is the largest revenue category.
Stand-alone advisory, staff augmentation, or technical consulting is the largest revenue category.
Software publishing, licensing, product resale, hosting, or infrastructure provisioning is the largest revenue category.
The establishment primarily performs on-site management and operation of a client's computer systems or data-processing facilities, which the NAICS definition assigns to 541513 rather than 541512.
No largest revenue category can be established, including an exact tie between categories.
alternatives.rationale: Base count is 30% of the normalized target band, rounded to the nearest firm, using a broader-channel survey estimate for managed services as the leading revenue source. Census reports 10,640 541512 establishments with IT infrastructure/network management revenue and 20,604 with IT technical-support revenue, but those establishment counts overlap and do not show which line is primary. Trade and association evidence also treats MSP, VAR, consultant, and systems-integrator labels as overlapping identities. The range therefore allows substantial hybrid reclassification and does not treat MSP as synonymous with NAICS 541512.

alternatives.name: IT consulting- or staffing-led specialist
inclusion_criteria: Employer firm is classified in NAICS 541512 and falls within the derived $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Stand-alone IT technical consulting, assessment, architecture advice, project management, or staff augmentation is the firm's largest revenue category.
Implementation may follow the advice, but project integration, recurring management/support, and products each contribute less revenue than consulting and staffing.
exclusion_criteria: Project-based systems design or integration is the largest revenue category.
Recurring managed or technical-support services are the largest revenue category.
Software publishing, licensing, product resale, hosting, or infrastructure provisioning is the largest revenue category.
No largest revenue category can be established, including an exact tie between categories.
alternatives.rationale: Base count is 14% of the normalized target band, a residual allocation after the two survey-anchored leading models and the separately bounded product-led model. The 2022 Economic Census reports 18,281 541512 establishments and about $18.8B of revenue associated with IT technical consulting, confirming a material consulting line but not primary-model firm counts. The low and high cases allow for consulting embedded in project firms and for firms using consultant labels while earning most revenue from another model.

alternatives.name: Product-, IP-, hosting-, or resale-led solution provider
inclusion_criteria: Employer firm is classified in NAICS 541512 and falls within the derived $1-10M EBITDA target band.
Software publishing, intellectual-property licensing, hosting or infrastructure provisioning, or integrated hardware and software resale is the firm's largest revenue category.
Systems-integration services remain sufficient for the firm to be classified in 541512 rather than wholesale, retail, software publishing, or data-processing industries.
exclusion_criteria: Project-based systems design or integration is the largest revenue category.
Recurring managed or technical-support services are the largest revenue category.
Stand-alone consulting or staff augmentation is the largest revenue category.
The firm only sells hardware or software without systems-integration services, which the NAICS definition assigns outside 541512.
No largest revenue category can be established, including an exact tie between categories.
alternatives.rationale: Base count is 7% of the normalized target band. Census identifies material 541512 revenue from application software publishing, hosting, infrastructure provisioning, licensing, and some hardware/software sales, while the NAICS boundary requires integration services when products are sold. Product incidence does not reveal the firm's primary model, and channel surveys show frequent resale by service firms, so a comparatively small base and a wide range are retained.

residual.name: Mixed, tied, or unclassified 541512 target-band firms
residual.rationale: Base residual is 5% of the 3,524-firm band, rounded so archetype bases plus residual equal the band total. It retains firms with tied or unobservable primary revenue categories, unusual combinations, or insufficient evidence for assignment. The high case recognizes that hybrid business models and multi-product Census reporting can prevent reliable primary-model classification.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Definition: 541512 Computer Systems Design Services
Economic Census: Products by Industry Statistics for the U.S.
2022 Economic Census Products by Industry downloadable dataset
CompTIA Releases 5th Annual Trends in Managed Services
sources.vintage: 2016 secondary summary of CompTIA channel survey

sources.title: 2022 State of the Channel: Happy Days Are Here Again
National Society of IT Service Providers: About

target_archetype.selection_basis: The four named archetypes assign hybrid firms by their single largest revenue category, making the options mutually exclusive; unresolved ties remain in the residual. Applied to the normalized 3,524-firm band, broader-channel leading-revenue shares give 1,551 project-led firms (44%) and 1,057 managed-services-led firms (30%). The difference is 494 firms, or 31.9% of the selected base, so there is no near tie on base counts. The project-led ranking is directionally corroborated by 2022 Census product revenue, where the enumerated design and integration lines total about $153.9B. Project-led is therefore the objective largest-base selection. The enumeration covers 3,348 firms at base, or 95.0% of the band, with the remaining 176 firms named as residual.

value_basis.scope: Employee and controllable-contractor cash costs used to deliver the selected project-led systems integrator's controlled value added. Hardware, software, cloud, carrier, and other third-party resale pass-through removed from controllable value-added revenue is excluded symmetrically from this cash-cost pool.
value_basis.controllable_value_added_boundary: R_cva equals recognized revenue minus documented third-party pass-through. For gross-presented hardware or software resale, deduct documented outside-provider acquisition cost once and retain the integrator's margin in R_cva. For net-presented resale, recognized margin is already value added and no second deduction is allowed. Every deducted amount is excluded symmetrically from employee cash cost, controllable-contractor cash cost, role pools, and G in the same scenario.

roles.title: Solution architecture and systems design
roles.rationale: Class-neutral structural allocation for discovery, requirements, solution architecture, security and network design, and technical specifications; excludes implementation and sales work assigned elsewhere.

roles.title: Implementation, configuration, and integration delivery
roles.rationale: Class-neutral structural allocation for employees and controlled contractors configuring, installing, integrating, migrating, and deploying client systems; excludes documented third-party resale pass-through.

roles.title: Project delivery management and quality assurance
roles.rationale: Class-neutral structural allocation for scoping control, scheduling, coordination, documentation, testing governance, and delivery quality; excludes technical build work and corporate management.

roles.title: Deployment support and user training
roles.rationale: Class-neutral structural allocation for go-live support, incident resolution tied to delivered systems, knowledge transfer, and user training; excludes recurring management that would determine the managed-services-led archetype.

roles.title: Sales, solution pursuit, and account management
roles.rationale: Class-neutral structural allocation for business development, proposal coordination, partner and vendor liaison, and account stewardship; excludes billable architecture and implementation.

roles.title: Executive, finance, people, compliance, and internal operations
roles.rationale: Class-neutral structural allocation for firm leadership and non-delivery operations, including finance, contracting, human resources, compliance, facilities, and internal systems; excludes project delivery management.

value_basis.rationale: The normalized dataset's role mix is available only for broad NAICS 5415 and is structurally mismatched to the selected project-led 541512 archetype, so it is rejected as a deterministic proxy. The two distinct cost identifiers separate employee cash cost from contractor cash cost under the operator's control. Six mutually exclusive project-led role groups allocate their combined pool and sum to 1.00. Every weight is a class-neutral ESTIMATE/NONE assumption with no source claim. Product acquisition and other outside-provider costs deducted as pass-through from recognized revenue remain outside both cost identifiers and every role allocation.
caveats: This assumption basis is unsupported evidence and must not be represented as observed, calculated, or proxy evidence.
Do not substitute the broad 5415 occupation mix or a managed-services staffing model.
Controllable contractor work is included only when the operator controls the delivered value added; a documented reimbursed outside-provider pass-through is excluded from both R_cva and V costs.
Hardware or software acquisition cost removed from gross recognized revenue may not also be included in the employee or contractor cash-cost pool.
The weights allocate the combined employee and controllable-contractor cash-cost pool; they do not establish either pool's dollar amount or share of revenue.

source_hints.source: Target-company revenue-recognition policy, audited general ledger, vendor invoices, project bills of materials, reseller agreements, and customer contracts
source_hints.why: Needed to establish recognized revenue, gross-versus-net presentation, documented hardware/software/cloud/carrier pass-through, and a positive internally consistent controllable value-added denominator without double counting.

source_hints.source: Target-company payroll, benefits, contractor ledgers, project time records, and role-mapped delivery costing for project-led integrators
source_hints.why: Needed to test or replace the frozen class-neutral assumption structure with target-specific cash costs while excluding every third-party amount already removed from revenue.

source_hints.source: Project-level case studies, systems-integration statements of work, implementation benchmarks, and operating surveys specific to mid-market project-led integrators
source_hints.why: Needed to distinguish architecture, build, project-control, support, and training work and establish implementation timing and constraints for the selected archetype.

source_hints.source: Audited segment disclosures, lender or transaction memoranda, and channel surveys reporting project backlog, repeat-client revenue, recurring support, resale margin, and customer concentration
source_hints.why: Project repeat business, recurring support, and retained resale margin have different retention implications; generic MSP retention is not transferable.

source_hints.source: Closed transaction databases and intermediary deal reports filtered to U.S. project-led systems integrators with approximately $1-10M EBITDA, including marketed, accessed, won, and closed counts
source_hints.why: Broad technology-services transaction counts combine software, staffing, consulting, VAR, and MSP models and do not establish executable or winnable targets for the selected archetype.

source_hints.source: Closed-deal EBITDA records, post-acquisition operating cases, certification and partner-program requirements, customer-contract change-of-control terms, and succession studies
source_hints.why: Needed to bound state-consistent entry and exit valuation and determine whether architecture authority, client trust, vendor status, and delivery oversight persist without the selling owner.

controllable_value_added: What recognized-revenue basis does the target use for hardware, software, cloud, carrier, and other third-party components, and which amounts are documented as gross-presented outside-provider pass-through?
For each base, low, and high case, does R_cva equal recognized revenue less documented pass-through, with net-presented reseller margin receiving no second deduction?

Can every hardware/software pass-through amount be reconciled to vendor invoices and customer contracts and excluded from the V cash-cost pool; if not, is R_cva MISSING rather than replaced by gross billings?
What target-specific evidence separates employee cash cost, controllable-contractor cash cost, and documented outside-provider pass-through for the selected project-led archetype?
Can mutually exclusive role weights and base/low/high cash-cost totals be bounded without the broad 5415 occupation mix; if not, which inputs must remain MISSING?
Is each contractor amount included exactly once as controlled delivery cost or excluded exactly once as denominator pass-through, with no category switching across scenarios?
operational_realization: For target-band project-led systems integrators, what workflow evidence separates solution architecture, implementation, project control, quality assurance, deployment support, and training costs?

What implementation durations, sequencing dependencies, security controls, vendor certifications, client-access constraints, and error-remediation requirements are supported by target-specific evidence?
Can five-year realization endpoints be bounded for project-led integrators rather than software publishers, pure consultants, or managed-services-led firms?
commercial_retention: What share of controllable value added comes from repeat projects, recurring support, managed services, retained resale margin, and first-time implementation work?
How do backlog conversion, project renewal, client concentration, vendor dependence, fixed-price exposure, and product pass-through affect commercial retention for the selected archetype?
Is retention measured on R_cva consistently across all scenarios rather than on gross billings that include excluded resale pass-through?
sale_availability: How many U.S. project-led systems integrators in the target EBITDA band were marketed, became accessible, were pursued, and were won in a defined recent period?
What supports each low, base, and high sale-availability endpoint after excluding pure MSPs, staffing firms, software companies, wholesalers, and firms outside the target band?
buyer_access_win: Can evidence exclude pure MSPs, staffing firms, software companies, wholesalers, and firms outside the target band while preserving a documented denominator?
What supports each availability, access, and win-share endpoint after accounting for add-ons, repeat sales, undisclosed deal size, and owner willingness?
valuation_robustness: What closed-deal EBITDA multiples apply to project-led systems integrators in the target band, and how do they vary with recurring revenue, backlog, concentration, product pass-through, and owner dependence?
Are earnings and revenue normalized consistently for contractor costs, owner compensation, working capital, and gross-versus-net resale presentation?
What evidence supports entry and downside-exit endpoints without importing software-company or MSP multiples?
operator_survival: Which sales, architecture, vendor-partner, delivery-governance, and client-escalation duties remain concentrated in the owner for target-band project-led integrators?
What observed succession or post-acquisition evidence shows whether those duties can transfer to a non-owner operator by year five?
Do client contracts, certifications, security clearances, partner statuses, or change-of-control provisions create target-specific survival constraints?

special_notes: This record describes only the selected project-led systems-integrator archetype and must not be presented as the economics of all NAICS 541512 firms.
MSP is not a synonym for NAICS 541512. Managed-services-led firms are a separate alternative, and on-site facilities management belongs in NAICS 541513 under the official definition.
Hybrid firms are assigned by their single largest revenue category; exact ties and firms with unobservable revenue mix remain in the residual.
Selection uncertainty remains true because project-led and managed-services-led supported count ranges overlap; the named alternatives cover 95% at base with an 80%-98% supported range.

Freeze pass-through identifier HARDWARE-SOFTWARE-RESALE-PASS-THROUGH: when hardware or software is recognized gross, deduct only documented third-party acquisition cost from recognized revenue; retain the integrator's margin in R_cva.
If hardware or software resale is recognized net, the recognized margin is already value added and HARDWARE-SOFTWARE-RESALE-PASS-THROUGH is zero; never deduct the product cost a second time.

Any amount deducted from revenue as hardware/software or other outside-provider pass-through is excluded from employee cash cost, controllable-contractor cash cost, every role pool, and G in the same base/low/high scenario.
Undocumented material pass-through may not be estimated away. If recognized revenue, accounting presentation, or pass-through cannot be positively and consistently bounded, R_cva and V are MISSING.
Economic Census product-line establishment counts overlap because establishments may report multiple products; they must not be summed as unique firms.

The broad 5415 occupation mix is rejected as a deterministic role proxy. The distinct employee and controllable-contractor cost IDs and six role weights are ESTIMATE/NONE assumptions with no source claim.
